narrative_style: Each item is a 1-3 sentence fact pattern resembling intake summary text. Drafted to plausibly touch 4-5 FOLIO concepts spanning multiple branches (Area of Law, Service, Actor, Damages, etc.) for rich auto-mapping plus ambiguity.
items: Plaintiff was rear-ended at a stoplight by a delivery van whose driver admitted to texting; she suffered whiplash, concussion symptoms, and ongoing chiropractic care, and is seeking compensation for medical bills, lost wages, and pain and suffering.

Client slipped on an unmarked wet floor near the produce section of a national grocery chain, fracturing her hip and requiring surgical repair followed by six months of physical therapy; store surveillance shows the spill was untreated for over an hour.

Mother of a deceased infant alleges the obstetrician failed to recognize fetal distress during labor, delaying an emergency C-section by 40 minutes; the child suffered hypoxic-ischemic encephalopathy and died at three days old.

Family was burned when a household pressure cooker exploded during normal use; the manufacturer had received 200 similar complaints but issued no recall. Plaintiffs seek damages for third-degree burns, disfigurement, and emotional distress.

Over 800 residents of a small town allege groundwater contamination from a nearby chemical plant caused elevated rates of childhood leukemia; epidemiological studies are pending and the defendant denies causation.

Pedestrian crossing in a marked crosswalk was struck by a left-turning sedan; the driver had a suspended license and prior DUI convictions. Plaintiff sustained a traumatic brain injury and now requires lifetime attendant care.

Tenant in a multi-family building tripped on a broken stair tread that the landlord had been notified about three weeks earlier; she suffered a fractured wrist requiring open reduction surgery and is unable to return to her bartending job.

Patient underwent a routine knee arthroscopy and was left with permanent nerve damage after the surgeon allegedly operated on the wrong leg; the hospital's incident report was never disclosed to the patient.

Driver of a compact SUV was killed when the vehicle's roof crushed during a low-speed rollover; the family alleges the roof structure failed federal safety standards and the manufacturer knew about the defect from prior testing.

A group of veterans claim long-term exposure to burn pits at overseas military installations caused respiratory cancers and autoimmune disorders; class certification is being sought against the federal contractor that operated the pits.

Motorcyclist was struck by an uninsured driver who fled the scene; plaintiff is seeking coverage under his own uninsured motorist policy after the carrier denied the claim on the grounds that the policy lapsed two days before the accident.

Hotel guest was sexually assaulted in her room by an intruder who entered through a hallway door that the property's electronic lock system failed to secure; security camera maintenance had been deferred for over a year.

Elderly nursing home resident developed stage-4 pressure ulcers and sepsis after staff repeatedly failed to reposition him; the facility was understaffed and had multiple prior citations from state regulators.

Children's playground equipment manufactured by defendant collapsed mid-use, injuring three minors and causing one to suffer a skull fracture; expert review suggests inadequate weld inspection during production.

Workers at a manufacturing plant developed mesothelioma decades after asbestos exposure from gaskets supplied by a now-bankrupt subcontractor; plaintiffs are pursuing claims against the plant owner under premises and product theories.
